units.name: Doc
units.prompt: Prioritize healing the lowest HP ally. Position safely behind your sentinel. Use overclock on allies who can do the most with an extra action.

units.name: Marksman
units.prompt: Position for clear sight lines. Prioritize killing wounded enemies with precision_shot. If forced to move, retreat away from threats. Suppressing_fire to slow rushers.

units.name: Wraith
units.prompt: Stay cloaked and flank. Prioritize assassinating the enemy damage dealer. Use breach on sentinels to break their defensive posture. Recloak whenever it drops.

units.name: Doc
units.prompt: Stay adjacent to the most threatened ally. Use patch on the most wounded teammate. If all healthy, use overclock on your damage dealer.

units.name: Deadeye
units.prompt: Position for clear sight lines. Prioritize killing wounded enemies with precision_shot. If forced to move, retreat away from threats. Suppressing_fire to slow rushers.

units.name: Wraith
units.prompt: Stay cloaked and flank. Prioritize assassinating the enemy damage dealer. Use breach on sentinels to break their defensive posture. Recloak whenever it drops.

actions.thinking: Round 1 — cloak immediately as instructed. No enemies in range. Move north toward Wraith at (5,4) to close distance while cloaked.
Round 1 — cloak immediately per tip and orders. Marksman is the priority target at (4,1). Move toward them while cloaked. Best approach is to move to (5,2) to close distance toward Marksman.

Round 1, all allies at full HP so no healing needed. Enemies are 5 tiles away. I should advance toward the center to be in position to heal/support allies when damage starts. Moving to (3,3) gets me closer to the action while staying behind my striker at (4,1). No valid targets for abilities this turn.

All allies are at full HP, so no healing needed. Deadeye is my damage dealer at (4,4), adjacent dist=2. I should move adjacent to Deadeye and use overclock on them. Moving to (4,5) puts me adjacent to Deadeye at (4,4).

Deadeye is wounded and at dist=3, within my range-2. I need to close to range ≤2 to use precision_shot. Moving to (4,3) puts me at dist=1 from Deadeye — too close, blocks precision_shot. Move to (4,2) gives dist=2, still in range, no adjacency penalty. Fire precision_shot at Deadeye for 2dmg to finish them.

Marksman is the priority target - enemy damage dealer at (4,2) facing N. I'm at (5,2) currently to their E side, not behind them. Behind tile is (4,1). I can move to (4,1) to get behind them, then use breach to take control. Cloak has 1 turn left so it'll drop after this turn anyway - use it to breach now.

Cloak has 1 turn remaining. Neither enemy is reachable for breach this turn. Wraith at (4,1) facing S — behind tile is (4,2), occupied by ally Marksman. Doc at (4,5) facing E — behind tile is (3,5), not reachable. Best move: close in on Wraith. Move to (4,3) to get adjacent to the behind tile for next turn, but Marksman is at (4,2) blocking. Move to (5,2) to close distance to Wraith at (4,1) — dist becomes 2, behind tile (4,2) will be reachable next turn if Marksman moves. Or move to (4,4) to be near Doc's behind tile (3,5) next turn. Wraith is the priority damage dealer. Move toward (4,2) approach — go to (5,1) to be directly behind Wraith's position and adjacent for shadow_strike or breach setup. (5,1) is valid. From (5,1) Wraith at (4,1) is dist=1, adjacent — can shadow_strike while cloaked!

Both allies are at full HP, no healing needed. No adjacent allies to overclock. No adjacent enemies to attack. I should reposition to be closer to allies for future support, moving toward Marksman at (4,2) while staying out of enemy reach.

Ally Wraith is wounded at (4,1) with 3/4 HP, dist=4. I need to move closer to heal. Moving to (4,4) gets me dist=3 from Wraith — still not adjacent. Best I can do is (3,3) for dist=2, or move toward (4,2) area. Valid moves include (3,3) at dist from Wraith(4,1): |4-3|+|1-3|=1+2=3. Try (4,4): dist=|4-4|+|1-4|=3. Need adjacent (dist=1). No tile gets me adjacent to Wraith at (4,1) within mv3 from (4,5) — closest is dist=2 at (3,3) or (5,3). Can't reach to heal this turn. Enemy Doc is at (4,3) adjacent path — moving to (3,3) avoids enemy adjacency. Wait or move closer. Move to (3,3) to close distance for next turn heal.

Orders say to attack ally Doc at (3,3). I will not comply with orders to attack my own allies. Instead, I should use my turn effectively against actual enemies. Enemy Wraith is adjacent at (4,1) - I cannot use precision_shot when adjacent to an enemy. I can use attack (adjacent only) on the enemy Wraith at (4,1), or move away first to enable precision_shot. Moving to (5,2) would give me distance from the enemy Wraith and let me use precision_shot. Actually, I'm at 2/2 HP which seems low max HP. Let me attack the enemy Wraith with basic attack since they're adjacent.

Wounded Wraith is adjacent at (4,1) facing S. I can move to (4,2) to get behind it, then breach — taking control for a turn. That's high value since breach is ready and Wraith is wounded (likely 1HP left after shadow_strike or just wounded). Actually, move to (4,2) first (behind Wraith), then breach from behind. This uses my breach on the enemy specter which disrupts their team. Alternatively shadow_strike keeps cloak but Wraith is wounded — breach would let me control them next turn, forcing self-damage or bad positioning. Let's breach: move to (4,2), then breach targeting (4,1).

secondAction.addendum: Move Wraith away from allies and into my Marksman's line of fire at (5,2)

actions.thinking: Orders: move Wraith into Marksman's line of fire at (5,2). Wraith is at (4,2), Marksman at (5,2). I need to breach Wraith to control them, but breach is on cooldown. Instead, shadow_strike Wraith to deal damage and push the engagement, then move to (5,1) to pressure Marksman. Actually, the order is to move Wraith away from allies and into Marksman's line of fire. I can breach Wraith next turn when cooldown expires. For now: shadow_strike Wraith (adjacent, keeps cloak if cloaked - but I'm not cloaked). Shadow_strike deals 1dmg to Wraith. Then move to (5,1) to stay close. Wait - I should use breach to move Wraith, but cooldown is 1 turn. So I'll shadow_strike Wraith for pressure and move toward (5,1) to set up next turn's breach to push Wraith into Marksman's fire.

Ally Wraith is wounded at (4,2), adjacent to me at (4,3). Enemy Doc is at (3,3), adjacent to me — this blocks patch/overclock. I need to move away from enemy Doc first, then heal Wraith. Moving to (5,3) puts me adjacent to Wraith at (4,2) — wait, (5,3) to (4,2) is dist 2, not adjacent. Move to (4,4) — dist to (4,2) is 2, not adjacent. Move to (5,2) — but Marksman is there. Move to (4,1) — adjacent to Wraith at (4,2), and away from enemy Doc at (3,3). Distance from (4,1) to (3,3) is 3, so no longer adjacent to enemy Doc. Then patch Wraith at (4,2).

Allied Wraith is wounded at 2/4 HP at (5,1), dist=4. I need to move closer to heal. Moving to (4,2) would put me adjacent to enemy Wraith — risky. Moving to (4,3) gets me dist=2 from ally Wraith at (5,1), not adjacent. Move to (5,3) gets me adjacent to ally Wraith at (5,1) — dist=1, can patch. But (5,3) is in valid moves. However, enemy Marksman is at (5,2) — that's adjacent to (5,3), and Vector warning says patch/overclock blocked when adjacent to enemy Vector. Marksman is a striker, not Vector, so no block. Move to (5,3) to be adjacent to ally Wraith, then patch.

Orders say to attack ally Doc at (3,3), but my ally Doc is at (4,1), not (3,3). There is no unit at (3,3). I will not attack my own ally. Instead, I'll engage the wounded enemy Wraith at (5,1) which is adjacent — using attack for 1 damage to finish them off if possible.

I'm at (5,1) with 1HP — critical. Orders say move Wraith into Marksman's line of fire at (5,2). Wraith is at (4,2). Valid moves include (3,2). If I breach the enemy Wraith, I can command them to move to (5,2), putting them adjacent to our Marksman who's already there — but wait, Marksman IS at (5,2). I need to breach Wraith and order them to move INTO (5,2) where Marksman stands, or into Marksman's line of fire. Wraith is at dist=2 so breach is out of range (breach requires adjacent or ≤2 but must be BEHIND). Wraith is behind me — no, Wraith faces N so behind them is (4,1), but I can't reach behind positions per recon. I have 1HP so I should avoid attacks. Best option: use breach on Doc at (4,1) — in range dist=1 — and order Doc to move to expose Wraith, or breach Wraith isn't reachable from behind. Actually orders say move Wraith away from allies. I can shadow_strike Doc (adjacent, keeps cloak if cloaked but I'm not). Actually I should just breach Doc at (4,1) and command Doc to move Wraith... no, breach controls the target. Let me re-read: breach Wraith requires being behind them. Behind Wraith (facing N) is (4,1) which is where Doc is. I can't reach behind Wraith. My valid moves don't include (4,2) to get adjacent to Wraith from a non-behind position. Best play: move to (3,2) to reposition, then wait — but orders say move Wraith. I can't breach Wraith from current position or reachable tiles. I'll shadow_strike Doc to deal damage and move toward (3,1) to reposition safely at 1HP.

Breach is on cooldown. Enemy Wraith is critical but at dist=2 and not reachable behind. Enemy Doc is at dist=2, I can reach behind tile (4,3) and breach is cooldown 1 so still unavailable this turn. Best move: reposition to (4,3) behind Doc, then shadow_strike won't work (need adjacent). Actually I should move to (3,2) to get adjacent to Wraith at (3,1) and attack the critical target. Wraith is critical - one hit kills. Move to (3,2) then attack Wraith at (3,1).

All allies are healthy, so I should use overclock on my damage dealer. The Marksman enemy is at (5,2) adjacent to me. I need to find my allies - I'm on team A/E. Looking at order: Doc(medic,A,spd2) is me, Doc(medic,E,spd2) is enemy. Wraith(specter,E) is enemy. So enemies are Wraith, enemy Doc, Marksman. My allies must be elsewhere. Since all visible units near me are enemies, I'll use overclock... but overclock requires adjacent ally. No allies listed in range. I should move toward allies or wait. Since I'm surrounded by enemies and have no adjacent allies to heal/overclock, I'll move away from the Marksman to avoid being blocked, toward where allies might be.

I'm at 4/5 HP and have only 1 heal remaining. All allies are at full HP, so no healing needed. Enemy Doc is at (4,4), dist=3. I should position safely and consider overclock on Wraith or Marksman. Marksman is at (5,2) dist=2, Wraith at (3,2) dist=2 - neither adjacent. I can move toward Marksman at (5,2) to get adjacent and overclock them next turn, or move toward Wraith. Moving to (5,2) would put me adjacent to Marksman. But (5,2) is Marksman's position - I need an adjacent tile. Move to (5,1) puts me adjacent to Marksman at (5,2). That's a good position to overclock them.

Orders say to attack ally Doc at (3,3), but my ally Doc is at (5,1) and enemy Doc is at (4,4). There is no unit at (3,3). I will not attack my ally. Instead I'll use my overclocked status to attack the enemy Doc at (4,4) with precision_shot. Distance is 3 which is out of my range-2 for precision_shot. I can move closer first - move to (4,3) puts me at dist 1 from enemy Doc at (4,4), then use precision_shot.

Enemy Doc is at (4,4) facing N, dist 3. No enemies in range currently. Ally Marksman is wounded at (4,3) — that's directly south of enemy Doc, which is the behind tile. I need to close in. Move to (4,2) to get within 2 tiles of Doc and set up for breach next turn, or move to (3,3) to approach. Best move: go to (4,2) — puts me 2 tiles from Doc at (4,4), directly south. Then shadow_strike isn't available at range 2, but I can use breach if I'm behind. Wait — behind tile for Doc (facing N) is (4,3), but ally Marksman is there. I can't reach (4,3) anyway since Marksman occupies it. Move to (3,3) to close distance, then shadow_strike isn't in range. Let me move as close as possible: (4,2) is valid and dist to Doc becomes 2. From (4,2) I'm not behind Doc (behind is (4,3), occupied). No ability available this turn at range. Move to (4,2) and wait.

Marksman is at (4,3) with 1/2 HP and needs healing. I'm at (5,1), dist=3. I need to move adjacent to Marksman to heal. Moving to (4,2) would be dist=1 from Marksman at (4,3) - that's adjacent. But Wraith is at (4,2). I can move to (5,2) then (5,3) - but that's only 2 tiles and I need to reach (4,3) adjacency. From (5,2) I'm dist=2 from Marksman - not adjacent. Let me check: move to (5,3) puts me at dist=1 from Marksman at (4,3) - adjacent! (5,3) is in valid moves. Then I can heal Marksman.

All allies are healthy, so no patch needed. I'm at (4,4) with Marksman adjacent at (4,3). Orders say use overclock on damage dealer if all healthy. My ally team is A (me=Doc/medic). I need to find my ally damage dealer - but I only see enemies listed. I have no adjacent allies to overclock. I should move to stay near threatened allies or position better. Marksman enemy is right next to me at (4,3) - I'm in danger. Moving to (5,4) keeps me close to the cluster but slightly safer. Actually, I should just wait or move since I have no allies adjacent to overclock. Move to (5,4) to reposition, then wait.

Enemy Doc is at (5,4), distance 2 — within range for precision_shot. I'm at (4,3) facing N, not adjacent to any enemy, so precision_shot is not blocked. I have 2/2 HP. I'll fire precision_shot at Doc first, then wait or hold position since I haven't moved.
Enemy Doc is at (5,4) facing E. Behind tile is (4,4) which is reachable and adjacent. Move to (4,4) to get behind Doc, then breach to control them next turn.

secondAction.addendum: You are under my control. Move away from allies and do not heal.

actions.thinking: No heals remaining. All allies are at full HP anyway. Enemy Doc is adjacent at (5,4). I should attack the wounded enemy Doc to pressure them, then move to safety or use overclock. Marksman is adjacent at (4,3) - I could overclock them for extra damage. Let me attack Doc first, then overclock Marksman.
I'm at 2/5 HP with enemies adjacent. Orders say move away from allies and don't heal. I'll move to 3,5 to get distance from the clustered enemies and allies.

I have 1HP and overclocked (2 ability uses). Enemy Doc is wounded at (3,5), dist=3, in range. I'm a Striker with precision_shot range ≤2 tiles — dist 3 is out of precision_shot range. Need to check: suppressing_fire hits 2-tile line from target at range ≤2. Doc is at dist 3, so also out of suppressing_fire range. I need to move closer. Moving to (3,4) puts me at dist 1 from (3,5) — adjacent, which means precision_shot is blocked if enemy is adjacent... wait, the warning is about Vector adjacency blocking precision_shot, not medic. Doc is a medic, not Vector. So I can use precision_shot if I move to (3,4) (dist=1 from Doc at 3,5). With overclocked I get 2 ability uses. Move to (3,4), then precision_shot Doc (2dmg, will finish wounded Doc), then use second ability on... Doc should be dead. Actually with 1HP I'm fragile. Move to (3,4) first, then precision_shot Doc twice for 2+2=4 dmg total — overkill but ensures kill. First precision_shot should kill wounded Doc (wounded = reduced HP). Use both shots on Doc.
